description: lib.rs:172-181: when write_all fails OR rename fails, code calls 'let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&tmp_path)'. If the remove fails (e.g., parent directory becomes read-only mid-operation, or filesystem-level error), a hidden 0600 tempfile containing the partially- or fully-written secret bytes is orphaned in the parent directory. This violates the failure-isolation invariant in WritableSecretStore docs: 'a failed write must not leave the secret in a corrupted or empty state' (the original is preserved, but a SECOND copy with the new secret is leaked). Mitigation: log the cleanup failure (no logging infrastructure in workspace yet — note as known caveat), or unlink the tempfile fd before write so it disappears on close (Linux O_TMPFILE pattern, then linkat). Minimum fix: doc the leak window in put() rustdoc.
title: [bug] tempfile cleanup is best-effort: 0600 file containing secret may be left in directory if remove_file fails
